module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can sign in with Email/Username and Password or initiate the forgot-password flow.
Line 2: Shows authentication success/failure messages, validation errors, and sign-in status.

Line 1: Users can create a new account by submitting the registration form with personal and credential data.
Line 2: Shows field-level validation errors and registration success confirmation.

Line 1: Users can view an accounts dashboard and attempt to navigate to individual account details.
Line 2: Shows a table of accounts with masked account numbers, account type, current balance, status, open date, and total balance.

Line 1: Users can open a new Checking or Savings account by selecting type, entering an initial deposit, and choosing a funding source.
Line 2: Shows account-type features, minimum deposit requirements, funding-account balance validation, real-time validation errors, and open-account confirmation.

Line 1: Users can initiate internal or external fund transfers by selecting transfer type, source account, destination, and amount.
Line 2: Shows transfer validation errors (sufficient funds, matching external account), transfer confirmations, and updated account balances.

Line 1: Users can submit bill payments by entering payee details, amount, and selecting a source account.
Line 2: Shows validation errors, payment confirmation with reference code, and updates to source-account balances.

Line 1: Users can apply for Personal, Auto, or Home loans by selecting type, entering loan and down payment amounts, and choosing collateral.
Line 2: Shows interest rates, amount-range validation, collateral and down-payment validation, approval/denial results, and loan confirmation.

Line 1: Users can edit and update contact/profile fields (name, address, phone) and save changes.
Line 2: Shows pre-filled profile fields, field-level validation errors, and profile update success confirmation.

Line 1: Users can request a new debit/credit card and manage existing card controls (spending limit, travel notices, freeze/unfreeze).
Line 2: Shows card-request confirmations with tracking IDs, list of existing cards with status, current spending limits, and travel notice details.

Line 1: Users can view a portfolio snapshot and execute fund trades (Buy or Sell) by selecting symbol, quantity, and account.
Line 2: Shows current fund holdings, market value, unrealized gain/loss, trade validation results, and trade execution confirmations.

Line 1: Users can generate account statements for a selected period and account, and toggle e-statement (paperless) preference.
Line 2: Shows retrieved transactions for the statement period, generation success/failure messages, and current e-statement preference/email.

Line 1: Users can change their password by providing the current password and a new strong password (with confirmation).
Line 2: Shows field validation errors, password-policy enforcement messages, and password-change success confirmation.

Line 1: Users can send secure support messages with optional attachments or request a callback with preferred date/time.
Line 2: Shows validation errors, sent-message confirmation with ticket ID, and scheduled callback request details/status.

test_cases.title: Successful sign in using email
test_cases.workflow: Submit the form to securely validate the provided credentials against the registered-user database
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user exists and is on the login page
steps: Enter a valid registered email address into the Email/Username field
Enter the corresponding valid password into the Password field
Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated, the system flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page

test_cases.title: Table footer displays total balance equal to sum of Current Balance values

Convert each Current Balance value to a numeric amount and calculate their sum
Read the total balance value displayed in the table footer
Compare the calculated sum to the footer total value
test_cases.expected_result: The footer total equals the sum of all Current Balance values shown in the table

test_cases.title: Rows are ordered by account creation date (earliest first)

Verify that the sequence of dates is in ascending order (earliest date first)
test_cases.expected_result: Account rows are ordered by Open Date with the earliest creation date appearing first

test_cases.title: Start date equal to today is rejected (not future)

test_cases.title: Change password successfully with valid current and strong new password
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Change Password collapsible panel is expanded
steps: Enter a valid current password into the Current Password field
Enter a valid strong password into the New Password field
Enter the same valid strong password into the Confirm New Password field
Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: System verifies the current password, updates credentials, displays 'Password changed successfully.' message, and indicates success (e.g., fields cleared or success state shown)

test_cases.title: Invalid current password prevents change and shows error

steps: Enter an incorrect value into the Current Password field (not the user's actual current password)
Enter a valid strong password into the New Password field
Enter the same valid strong password into the Confirm New Password field
Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: System rejects the change, displays an error indicating the current password is incorrect, and highlights the Current Password field

test_cases.title: New Password and Confirm New Password mismatch displays validation error

Enter a different password (that does not match) into the Confirm New Password field
Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is shown indicating New Password and Confirm New Password must match and the mismatched field(s) are highlighted; password change is not processed

Verify the New Password field is present and enabled
Verify the Confirm New Password field is present and enabled
Verify the Change Password button is present and enabled
test_cases.expected_result: All three password fields and the Change Password button are visible and enabled when the panel is expanded

test_cases.title: Current Password field empty displays validation error

Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is shown and the Current Password field is highlighted indicating it is required; password change is not processed

test_cases.title: New Password field empty displays validation error
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the Change Password collapsible panel is expanded
